passages.passage_text: Directions: A statement is given followed by three courses of action. A course of action is taken for improvement, follow-up etc. Read the statement carefully and pick the correct answer choice.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Financial stringency prevented the State Government from paying salaries to its employees for the last three months. Courses of action: I. The State Government should reduce its expenditure and arrange to pay the salaries to the employees. II. The State Government should immediately call off the staff strength.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The Finance Minister submits his resignation a month before his new budget is to be presented in the Parliament. Courses of action: I. The resignation should be accepted and another person should be appointed as the Finance Minister. II. The resignation should not be accepted.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The librarian finds some cases in which the pages from the books issued from the library are torn. Courses of action: I. The librarian should keep a record of books taken by each student and if the pages are found missing, strict measures should be taken against the guilty person who had been issued that book. II. Some fines should be collected from the students to collectively renovate the library.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The killer microbe was so far claimed 100 lives in ten tribal villages in M.P. during the past three weeks. Courses of action: I. The residents of these villages should immediately be shifted to a non-infected area. II. The Government should immediately send a medical squad to this area to restrict spread of the killer disease.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Although advancement in medical sciences has raised life expectancy, the rate of child mortality remains high in India. Courses of action: I. Efforts should be made to lower life expectancy. II. Medical scientists should be asked to give up their jobs. III. Efforts should be made to decrease the rate of child mortality.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: A new case of fraud and misappropriation of Government fund has been detected in a certain state. Courses of action: I. The case should be fully investigated. II. The government should reconsider the mode of appropriation of its funds. III. Officials concerned with the fraud should be punished.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Rapid growth of urban population has led to a severe shortage of living space resulting in a drop in living conditions. Courses of action: I. The Government should draw plans for urban regulation and development. II. All village-to-town migrations should be stopped. III. The Government should demolish all five-star hotels and build people's cottages there.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The police has failed to disclose the elderly citizens of the society. Only last week three old persons were killed at three different places. Courses of action: I. The police should be asked to take special care of women and children. II. The Government should bring a legislation against the killing of elderly people. III. Criminals who are accused of killing elderly people should be prosecuted by the state.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Passengers travelling by the Indian Railways face a lot of hardships and inconveniences. Courses of action: I. Efforts should be made to stop overcrowding in trains. II. Facilities for safe and pure drinking water should be provided. III. The railway stations and platforms should be made more clean and hygienic.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The problem of female infanticide has still not disappeared from our society.
